class.name: GLTFBufferView
class.description: GLTFBufferView is a data structure representing a glTF [code]bufferView[/code] that would be found in the [code]"bufferViews"[/code] array. A buffer is a blob of binary data. A buffer view is a slice of a buffer that can be used to identify and extract data from the buffer. Most custom uses of buffers only need to use the [member buffer], [member byte_length], and [member byte_offset]. The [member byte_stride] and [member indices] properties are for more advanced use cases such as interleaved mesh data encoded for the GPU.

link.title: Buffers, BufferViews, and Accessors in Khronos glTF specification

method.name: from_dictionary
method.description: Creates a new GLTFBufferView instance by parsing the given [Dictionary].

method.name: load_buffer_view_data
method.description: Loads the buffer view data from the buffer referenced by this buffer view in the given [GLTFState]. Interleaved data with a byte stride is not yet supported by this method. The data is returned as a [PackedByteArray].

method.name: to_dictionary
method.description: Serializes this GLTFBufferView instance into a [Dictionary].

method.name: write_new_buffer_view_into_state
method.description: Writes the given [param input_data] into a buffer view in the given [GLTFState] with its start byte offset aligned to a multiple of [param alignment]. This is usually a new buffer view, but may refer to an existing buffer view if an identical buffer view already exists and [param deduplicate] is [code]true[/code] (default). When a new buffer view is created, [param buffer_index] determines which buffer the new buffer view will point to, which is buffer 0 by default.

members.member: The index of the buffer this buffer view is referencing. If [code]-1[/code], this buffer view is not referencing any buffer.
The length, in bytes, of this buffer view. If [code]0[/code], this buffer view is empty.
The offset, in bytes, from the start of the buffer to the start of this buffer view.
The stride, in bytes, between interleaved data. If [code]-1[/code], this buffer view is not interleaved.

[code]true[/code] if the GLTFBufferView's OpenGL GPU buffer type is an [code]ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER[/code] used for vertex indices (integer constant [code]34963[/code]). [code]false[/code] if the buffer type is any other value. See [url=https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF-Tutorials/blob/master/gltfTutorial/gltfTutorial_005_BuffersBufferViewsAccessors.md]Buffers, BufferViews, and Accessors[/url] for possible values. This property is set on import and used on export.

[code]true[/code] if the GLTFBufferView's OpenGL GPU buffer type is an [code]ARRAY_BUFFER[/code] used for vertex attributes (integer constant [code]34962[/code]). [code]false[/code] if the buffer type is any other value. See [url=https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF-Tutorials/blob/master/gltfTutorial/gltfTutorial_005_BuffersBufferViewsAccessors.md]Buffers, BufferViews, and Accessors[/url] for possible values. This property is set on import and used on export.

constants.constant: Indicates that the buffer view does not have a target. Non-mesh buffer views should use this target, and mesh buffer views should use either [constant TARGET_ARRAY_BUFFER] or [constant TARGET_ELEMENT_ARRAY_BUFFER].
Indicates that the buffer view is an array buffer, which contains mesh vertex attributes such as vertex positions, normals, UVs, etc.
Indicates that the buffer view is an element array buffer, which contains mesh vertex indices.
